  • Physics Module (PyC)
  • View page source

Physics Module (PyC)

The PyC physics module provides high-performance physics calculations.

Overview

Located in pyc/pyc/physics/, this module implements common particle physics calculations with optimized C++ and CUDA backends.

API Reference

Delta R Calculation

DeltaR(eta1, phi1, eta2, phi2)

Calculate angular distance (ΔR) between two particles.

Parameters:

  • eta1 – Pseudorapidity of first particle

  • phi1 – Azimuthal angle of first particle (radians)

  • eta2 – Pseudorapidity of second particle

  • phi2 – Azimuthal angle of second particle (radians)

Returns:

ΔR = √(Δη² + Δφ²)

Return type:

float or torch.Tensor

\[\Delta R = \sqrt{(\eta_1 - \eta_2)^2 + (\phi_1 - \phi_2)^2}\]

Example:

import pyc.physics as phys

dr = phys.DeltaR(jet_eta, jet_phi, lepton_eta, lepton_phi)

Invariant Mass

InvariantMass(pt1, eta1, phi1, E1, pt2, eta2, phi2, E2)

Calculate invariant mass of two particles.

Parameters:

  • pt1 – Transverse momentum of first particle [GeV]

  • eta1 – Pseudorapidity of first particle

  • phi1 – Azimuthal angle of first particle

  • E1 – Energy of first particle [GeV]

  • pt2 – Transverse momentum of second particle [GeV]

  • eta2 – Pseudorapidity of second particle

  • phi2 – Azimuthal angle of second particle

  • E2 – Energy of second particle [GeV]

Returns:

Invariant mass [GeV]

Return type:

float or torch.Tensor

\[m_{inv} = \sqrt{(E_1 + E_2)^2 - |\vec{p}_1 + \vec{p}_2|^2}\]

Example:

mass = phys.InvariantMass(
    jet1.pt, jet1.eta, jet1.phi, jet1.E,
    jet2.pt, jet2.eta, jet2.phi, jet2.E
)

Transverse Mass

TransverseMass(pt1, phi1, pt2, phi2)

Calculate transverse mass.

Parameters:

  • pt1 – Transverse momentum of first particle [GeV]

  • phi1 – Azimuthal angle of first particle

  • pt2 – Transverse momentum of second particle [GeV]

  • phi2 – Azimuthal angle of second particle

Returns:

Transverse mass [GeV]

Return type:

float or torch.Tensor

\[m_T = \sqrt{2 p_{T,1} p_{T,2} (1 - \cos(\phi_1 - \phi_2))}\]

Four-Vector Operations

FourVector(pt, eta, phi, E)

Create a 4-vector from kinematic variables.

Parameters:

  • pt – Transverse momentum [GeV]

  • eta – Pseudorapidity

  • phi – Azimuthal angle

  • E – Energy [GeV]

Returns:

4-vector (px, py, pz, E)

Return type:

tuple or torch.Tensor

AddFourVectors(vec1, vec2)

Add two 4-vectors.

Parameters:

  • vec1 – First 4-vector

  • vec2 – Second 4-vector

Returns:

Sum 4-vector

Return type:

torch.Tensor

Angular Utilities

DeltaPhi(phi1, phi2)

Calculate difference in azimuthal angle, accounting for 2π periodicity.

Parameters:

  • phi1 – First angle [radians]

  • phi2 – Second angle [radians]

Returns:

Δφ in range [-π, π]

Return type:

float or torch.Tensor

DeltaEta(eta1, eta2)

Calculate difference in pseudorapidity.

Parameters:

  • eta1 – First pseudorapidity

  • eta2 – Second pseudorapidity

Returns:

Δη

Return type:

float or torch.Tensor

Batch Operations

All functions support batch operations with tensors:

import torch
import pyc.physics as phys

# Batch of 1000 particle pairs
eta1 = torch.randn(1000)
phi1 = torch.randn(1000)
eta2 = torch.randn(1000)
phi2 = torch.randn(1000)

# Compute all delta R values at once
dr = phys.DeltaR(eta1, phi1, eta2, phi2)  # shape: (1000,)

GPU Acceleration

Operations automatically use GPU when tensors are on GPU:

import torch
import pyc.physics as phys

# Move to GPU
eta1_gpu = eta1.cuda()
phi1_gpu = phi1.cuda()
eta2_gpu = eta2.cuda()
phi2_gpu = phi2.cuda()

# Computation runs on GPU
dr_gpu = phys.DeltaR(eta1_gpu, phi1_gpu, eta2_gpu, phi2_gpu)

Implementation

The physics module is implemented in:

  • physics.cxx: C++ CPU implementation

  • include/physics/physics.h: C++ header

  • cuda/physics.cxx: CUDA GPU implementation

Performance

Typical speedups over numpy (CPU):

  • Delta R: 50-100x faster

  • Invariant mass: 30-80x faster

  • Batch operations: 100-1000x faster on GPU

See Also

  • Operators Module (PyC) - Tensor operations

  • Transform Module (PyC) - Coordinate transformations
